Usage: yt-dlp [OPTIONS] URL [URL...]

General Options:
    -h, --help                      Print this help text and exit
    --version                       Print program version and exit
    -U, --update                    Update this program to the latest version
    -i, --ignore-errors             Ignore download and postprocessing errors
    --abort-on-error                Abort downloading of further videos if an
                                    error occurs
    --no-abort-on-error             Continue with next video on download errors
    -q, --quiet                     Activate quiet mode
    --no-warnings                   Ignore warnings
    -s, --simulate                  Do not download the video and do not write
                                    anything to disk

Network Options:
    --proxy URL                     Use the specified HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS proxy
    --socket-timeout SECONDS        Time to wait before giving up, in seconds
    -4, --force-ipv4                Make all connections via IPv4
    -6, --force-ipv6                Make all connections via IPv6

Video Selection:
    -I, --playlist-items ITEM_SPEC  Comma separated playlist_index of the items
                                    to download
    --min-filesize SIZE             Abort download if the file is smaller than
                                    SIZE (e.g. 50k or 44.6M)
    --max-filesize SIZE             Abort download if the file is larger than SIZE
    --date DATE                     Download only videos uploaded on this date
    --no-playlist                   Download only the video, if the URL refers
                                    to a video and a playlist
    --yes-playlist                  Download the playlist, if the URL refers to
                                    a video and a playlist

Download Options:
    -N, --concurrent-fragments N    Number of fragments of a dash/hlsnative
                                    video to download concurrently (default is 1)
    -r, --limit-rate RATE           Maximum download rate in bytes per second
                                    (e.g. 50K or 4.2M)
    -R, --retries RETRIES           Number of retries (default is 10), or
                                    "infinite"

Filesystem Options:
    -a, --batch-file FILE           File containing URLs to download, one URL
                                    per line
    -P, --paths TYPES:PATH          The paths where the files should be
                                    downloaded
    -o, --output TEMPLATE           Output filename template
    --restrict-filenames            Restrict filenames to only ASCII characters
    -w, --no-overwrites             Do not overwrite any files
    -c, --continue                  Resume partially downloaded files (default)
    --cookies FILE                  Netscape formatted file to read cookies from
                                    and dump cookie jar in

Verbosity and Simulation Options:
    --print TEMPLATE                Field name or output template to print to
                                    screen
    -j, --dump-json                 Quiet, but print JSON information for each
                                    video
    -v, --verbose                   Print various debugging information

Video Format Options:
    -f, --format FORMAT             Video format code, see "FORMAT SELECTION"
    -S, --format-sort SORTORDER     Sort the formats by the fields given
    --video-multistreams            Allow multiple video streams to be merged
                                    into a single file
    -F, --list-formats              List available formats of each video
    --merge-output-format FORMAT    Containers that may be used when merging
                                    formats (e.g. mp4, mkv, webm)

Subtitle Options:
    --write-subs                    Write subtitle file
    --write-auto-subs               Write automatically generated subtitle file
    --sub-langs LANGS               Languages of the subtitles to download
                                    (e.g. "en.*,ja")

Post-Processing Options:
    -x, --extract-audio             Convert video files to audio-only files
    --audio-format FORMAT           Format to convert the audio to when -x is
                                    used (best, aac, flac, mp3, m4a, opus, wav)
    --audio-quality QUALITY         Specify ffmpeg audio quality to use when
                                    converting audio (0 best to 10 worst)
    --embed-subs                    Embed subtitles in the video
    --embed-thumbnail               Embed thumbnail in the video as cover art
    --embed-metadata                Embed metadata to the video file
    -k, --keep-video                Keep the intermediate video file on disk
                                    after post-processing
